scope: Release APK real Android system picker 24 MiB user-origin attachment upload/download loopback through a server-wide gateway.

commits.message: test: add release upload system picker smoke
test: reverse gateway port for proxied release smokes

source.head: 7e436f7e
source.commit: fix: order mobile native transcript pages by record time

release_apk.build_warning: file_picker and mobile_scanner still use plugin-applied Kotlin Gradle Plugin; Flutter reports this as a future deprecation warning.

ui_smoke.automation: ADB UIAutomator XML plus adb input tap; real Android DocumentsUI system picker; no Flutter Driver.

acceptance_notes: The run started two disposable real /home/bfly/yunwei/test_ccb2 projects and a loopback-only server-wide gateway.
The release APK was seeded with the paired profile, installed, and launched without Flutter Driver.

ADB UIAutomator opened test_ccb2_alpha/mobile_probe from the real server-wide project list, tapped the attachment button, selected File, and picked the 24 MiB text document from Android DocumentsUI Recent files.
The app uploaded the user-origin file through the selected-agent composer, rendered the resulting conversation attachment chip, tapped it, and showed the Saved snackbar after downloading it back.
The request-counting proxy observed one POST file upload and one GET file download; the downloaded 25,165,824 bytes matched the uploaded SHA256.
The harness installed adb reverse for both the request proxy port and the underlying gateway port, so terminal/session routes did not produce connection-refused noise.
Device metrics after a 5-second post-save settle stayed below the PSS growth gate and had no app wake locks, no FATAL/ANR/OOM, and no skipped-frame markers.
residual_risks: This closes release-mode user-origin upload with the real Android system picker for the local real server-wide Android Emulator path.
Physical-device/Tailnet transfer pressure remains a separate validation path before broader external release.
The Flutter build still reports future Kotlin Gradle Plugin deprecation warnings for file_picker and mobile_scanner.
